event_world_42_20260423_084415_0000.name: Folk & Roots Gathering
event_world_42_20260423_084415_0000.description: Celebrate the roots of acoustic music with storytelling songwriters from across the globe, sharing ballads, blues, and bluegrass on an intimate outdoor stage.

reviews.text: The concept was interesting but execution felt rushed and under-rehearsed.
Too many commercial sponsors with their logos dominating every surface — completely killed the atmosphere.
The app kept showing wrong tent locations and staff gave contradictory directions.
Middle-of-the-road experience — worth attending once but not something to travel specifically for.

event_world_42_20260423_084415_0001.name: Cherry Blossom Night Market
event_world_42_20260423_084415_0001.description: Celebrate peak bloom with a twilight market under illuminated sakura trees — traditional sake, street food, and live shamisen performances throughout.

reviews.text: The rain shelter was woefully inadequate and the ground turned to mud after an hour.
Beautifully curated programme with a strong balance of emerging and established artists.
Average experience — neither the worst nor the best event I have attended this year.
Confusing layout and poor signage made it hard to navigate between stages.

event_world_42_20260423_084415_0002.name: Wine & Cheese Masterclass
event_world_42_20260423_084415_0002.description: A Michelin-starred sommelier guides guests through rare bottles paired with artisan cheeses, revealing the science and art behind perfect flavor harmony.

reviews.text: Terrible organisation — queues stretched around the block and we missed the first act entirely.
Middle-of-the-road experience — worth attending once but not something to travel specifically for.

event_world_42_20260423_084415_0003.name: Artisan Crafts & Vintage Fair
event_world_42_20260423_084415_0003.description: Over 200 stalls of handmade jewellery, vintage clothing, rare books, and bespoke homeware from independent makers across the region.

reviews.text: Terrible organisation — queues stretched around the block and we missed the first act entirely.

event_world_42_20260423_084415_0004.name: Classical Gala Evening
event_world_42_20260423_084415_0004.description: The City Philharmonic performs Beethoven's 9th Symphony and Rachmaninoff's Piano Concerto No. 2 in a breathtaking candlelit hall.

reviews.text: The concept was interesting but execution felt rushed and under-rehearsed.

reviews.text: Unforgettable night — the headline act played three encores and the crowd energy was incredible.
Parking chaos added two hours to our evening and the toilet facilities were disgraceful.
Performers were 90 minutes late and the cancellation announcement came via a single tweet.
Half the scheduled activities were moved indoors with no explanation; the outdoor programme was gutted.
Overcrowded beyond safe limits; I had to leave early as the crowd pressure near the stage felt dangerous.
Smooth entry with QR code scanning; the warm-up act was surprisingly better than expected.
Terrible organisation — queues stretched around the block and we missed the first act entirely.

event_world_42_20260423_084415_0039.name: Electronic Beats Festival
event_world_42_20260423_084415_0039.description: Three stages, twelve DJs, and a laser show that runs all night. Top acts from Berlin's underground scene headline this pulse-pounding electronic showcase.

reviews.text: Beautifully curated programme with a strong balance of emerging and established artists.
Performers were 90 minutes late and the cancellation announcement came via a single tweet.
The event started ninety minutes late with no explanation or apology from the organisers.
Decent show but the venue's sightlines were poor from the back rows.
The venue's natural amphitheatre setting made even quieter moments feel dramatic and resonant.
Long waits at every food and drink stall; the cashless payment system crashed repeatedly.
Smooth entry with QR code scanning; the warm-up act was surprisingly better than expected.
Middle-of-the-road experience — worth attending once but not something to travel specifically for.

event_world_42_20260423_084415_0040.name: International Stand-Up Night
event_world_42_20260423_084415_0040.description: Six comedians from six continents take the stage for a two-hour showcase of wildly different humour styles — slapstick, dark comedy, and sharp political satire.

reviews.text: Intimate venue setting meant even the cheap tickets gave a brilliant view of the stage.
The concept was interesting but execution felt rushed and under-rehearsed.

event_world_42_20260423_084415_0041.name: Lantern Sky Festival
event_world_42_20260423_084415_0041.description: Thousands of hand-painted sky lanterns are released simultaneously at dusk in a breathtaking tradition that lights up the entire horizon.

event_world_42_20260423_084415_0042.description: Celebrate peak bloom with a twilight market under illuminated sakura trees — traditional sake, street food, and live shamisen performances throughout.

event_world_42_20260423_084415_0043.name: Organic Farmers Market
event_world_42_20260423_084415_0043.description: Every Sunday, local farmers bring fresh seasonal produce, homemade preserves, and foraged ingredients directly to city-centre buyers — no middlemen, all flavour.

event_world_42_20260423_084415_0044.name: Ramen Showdown
event_world_42_20260423_084415_0044.description: Ten ramen chefs compete head-to-head for the title of best bowl in the city. Guests vote, judges deliberate, and everyone leaves full.

event_world_42_20260423_084415_0046.description: The world-sensation aespa is in town! Get ready for an unforgettable night of dynamic electronic dance music and stunning holographic visuals.

reviews.text: Spectacular light show and the best sound quality I have ever heard live.

event_world_42_20260423_084415_0049.description: A boundary-pushing director stages Shakespeare's Hamlet in a dystopian near-future, with a gender-swapped cast and an original score by an electronic composer.
